title: Anchor Hocking Triple Pour Glass Measuring Cup, 8 Oz Measuring Glass
description:
About this item
INCLUDES: This Anchor Hocking measuring cup set contains (1) 8-ounce measuring cup. 3-pour design to make your measuring easier and more precise than ever.
USE: These glass measuring cups make perfect wedding gifts, holiday gifts, and birthday gifts. Thick walled and sturdy based, prep easier than ever with the more reliable measuring cup.
SAFE: Anchor Hocking glass is healthier than plastic and metal. It will not warp, stain, retain smells, or leach dangerous chemicals into food or drinks.
FIVE-YEAR : Backed by a protective limited five-year , these glass measuring cups promise to impress for many years to come.
AMERICAN MADE GLASS SINCE 1905
reviews_5s:
The good: the measurements are hard-etched into the glass so there is nothing to wear-off or wash off. The glass will last for years as long as you don't break the glass..and the measurement marking will stay on for years. Obviously since there is no paint involved, the measurements will forever withstand dishwasher and/or any kind of washer. Perfect!!

It's a slim measuring cup (unlike a "cup" shape measuring cup that is wide). Because it is slim, the measurements are slightly more accurate because a little change in volume will show up more clearly in a slimmer, taller measuring device than a short, fat, wide measuring device. Simple physics. So for more precise measuring, I use this one. For non-precise measuring, I use my regular measuring cups.

Now the lid. . You can read other people's review about the lid and I have to agree as well: the lid is just... for show. It is not secure, not sealing, and does not work like a lid as if this is a tupperware or something. The lid is more for.. show. or allow you to stack something on top of the measuring cup, maybe.

So, have the correct expectation up front: the measuring cup works great! The measurements are great and will never come off! It slim so it's great! And the lid--just throw it away and pretend it didn't come with a lid then you will end up feeling great!! (lol)

The triple-pour spout... eh, it doesn't add much nor subtract anything. I haven't been wow'ed by the triple-pour spouts, nor does missing it cause me to feel regretful.

Hope this review helped! :DI love this little measure. There is a reason that lab ware is shaped the way it is: the lab-inspired narrow shape of this makes it much easier to get an accurate measure because a small change in the amount makes a larger change in the height. The embossed markings don't come off in the dishwasher - like many other similar measures I have used. The pour spouts are crisp and seem much better than my Pyrex measuring cups at avoiding the teapot effect - where the liquid runs down the outside when you pour it.

One other reviewer checked theirs against their Pyrex measuring cup and concluded that the measure on this may not be accurate. I skipped the Pyrex, likely to be off itself, and checked directly using plain water and a scale. I found that 8 ounces of water was exactly at the one cup mark when read at eye level. The other marks show similarly with 1/4 cup at 2 ounces and 1/2 cup at 4 ounces.

I like having the multiple scales. I occasionally cook to a recipe from abroad and the availability of metric is really nice. I can read in any units without having to be ambidextrous because there is a pour spout for each set of markings.

The lid is a storage lid and does not seal well enough to use it for shaking but is quite nice to be able to pre-measure ingredients and keep them refrigerated until ready to use. The glass is not tempered so it won't take thermal shock, i.e., don't put something very hot in it and then something very cold or use it in the microwave. It is actually stamped "no microwave" on the bottom.

Since my use is for measuring liquids, this suits my needs very well.I ordered this for my friend. I already have one and love mine. The numbers are etched on the glass, so cannot be washed off. Glass is thick and good quality. The only thing for me is the lid does not stay on due to the round corners on the top. I don't use the lid, but it cost the same with or without it, so I got it just in case my friend wanted it. If you are looking for the lid to stay on this one is not for you. Otherwise it is an awesome measuring cup.I am so pleased with this purchase! This measuring glass is sturdier, heavier, and thicker glass than I expected which I am very pleased about - it has a very substantial feel in my hand. I love having the 3 spouts so I can pour easily in whatever direction I happen to be holding it. It has a good feel in my hands, just the right size to grasp and hold. I love having raised markings that are part of the glass itself. To me, it has almost a vintage appearance which I like. It is true that the lid does not snap on easily or tightly but that is not crucial for me and I am sure I will still use it occasionally. I am puzzled by the instructions that say do not use in microwave as it seems as sturdy as other glass measuring cups I have used. In fact, on the bottom it says "not for kitchen use" --what?? I have used it in the microwave to melt butter a few times with no problem.
In spite of these quirks, I am totally taken with this cute 1 cup measure and am using it often and happily!!First of all, Anchor Hocking is an American company that has made high quality glass products - in America - for over a 100 years. This design was fairly unique, and Amazon dropped the price on it so I jumped on it. It's not very big, but I found it works well as a compact container for storing leftover pancake mix. One of the reasons it works so well for left over pancake mix is due to the lid! The uniquely shaped lid on my container actually fits quite well. I noticed a lot of other people complaining about their lids not fitting well... but that was not MY experience. All of that said, I'm thinking that you may be best off by NOT placing the lid in a hot dishwasher (for fear the heat could cause a mild change in the plastic). So, I'd recommend hand-washing the lid, only, while the rest of the glass container would be fine to stick in the dishwasher.It's a handy size, and weight, easy to read, pour. It's accurate, and good price.I didn’t know what to expect with things being made so cheaply these days. Not this item, extremely thick with three pour spout and a top. Each spout having a different type measurement for liquids. This is the one for cold to cool temperatures. I wouldn’t use for hot temperatures as this 8 ounce measuring cup is to be held. Great buy!!Needed a single eight ounce measuring cup, and came across this one. All of the Anchor Hocking products I have bought have all been absolutely awesome, and this is of their same typical quality i am used to from them. Made of good quality and very good for the price. A definite recommend.I love it so much, I bought 10 more to give as gifts. I would say this is one of my favorite things.
I only wish it was a two-cup capacity.Great price fast shipping thank you so much.
reviews_1s:
This was a replacement for one I'd broken so I know how it should look and perform. I love this triple pour measuring glass and was appalled when I saw what was delivered. The lid was filthy and the glass cup itself is obviously poor seconds; the pouring spouts are irregular, and the top edge of the glass looks uneven with a sort of pitting at the top. I couldn't take a photo that did justice to the abysmal quality. I am returning and if I re-order will do so from another supplier or simply order directly from Anchor Hocking.Fact - this is pressed glass, Not Borosilicate glass (Pyrex), therefore, not microwaveable. The first Triple Pour Glass Measuring Cup, 8 oz, I received had a very visible, large crack through the bottom and up the side. There was no damage to the boxes it came in. I requested a replacement. The second one has a small crack at the top, and again both boxes they were shipped in were not damaged. I've returned them. Maybe I'll try again in a few months. BTW, it was on sale for $3.97.I have the exact same item, purchased 6 years ago. This new one looks like it was made by 5 year olds. Side by side it is hilarious. And painful, to think that Anchor Hocking is now producing such complete garbage. It isn’t even straight. The numbers are big and blurry, compared to the sharp precise numbers produced by the old mold. And the bottom is uneven, where as the old one is perfect: straight & precise. Which is what you expect in a measuring tool. I wouldn’t even give 1 star except I have toAfter a couple of years of use crack and break. Not good at things like melting butter in the microwave they because they break under the heat. Just replacing with Pyrex brand...Bought to use as a shaker jar....... . disaster. Lid doesn't seal.😡.This product looks awesome on paper and first glance. The markings are sufficiently readable and engraved, rather than printed, so I don't have to worry about them rubbing off within a few years.

Unfortunately one of the spouts broke off the first time I put the measuring glass through the dishwasher. This is seriously disconcerting, because the product was marketed as dishwasher safe, and I've put plenty of much more fragile glassware (wine glasses) through the washer without issues.

Unfortunately I am past the warranty and can no longer return this product.Had to return. Based on other reviews I didn’t put them in dishwasher, but even handwashing gently removed all the markings. Nothing is etched or engraved in the glass, so once the markings are gone they are useless for measuring. Very disappointed as the quality of the glass seemed good. If they had been engraved I would have kept them even if the marker had worn off. I really want a way to measure small amounts of liquid and these disappointed and were returned.It said on the bottom not to microwave it, which is one of the reasons I wanted it, so I returned it. Seems odd to make a glass measuring cup that can't go in the microwave.This ended up being much heavier than I thought it would be. Didn’t know that a lid was included and the fact that it does not have a good fit does not matter to me. No idea why it has 3 pouring spouts, i just needed a 1 cup glass measuring cup. How difficult could that be? I noticed after I took it out of the box that there was a very rough edge at the bottom edge of it. See pics above, its hard to tell but it’s there. Sending this back and will get something else. Also never heard of a glass measuring cup that could not go in the microwave.The lid is completely ineffective.
